destinations.evidence: The builder predicate allows no-job, all-settled-or-expired, and fulfillment cases, and skips only non-fulfillment transactions from still-locked senders.
The differential harness directly compares the builder predicate against a translation of verifier nonce-lock phases across the same sender/job snapshot.

The verifier rejects regular transactions from senders with active unsettled jobs unless the transaction hash itself is an existing fulfillment job, matching the registry predicate tested by the harness.

equivalence_argument: The port keeps the nonce-lock decision rule in a shared registry helper and has an explicit differential harness proving equivalence with the verifier phases for no jobs, settled/expired jobs, fulfillment transactions, non-fulfillment locked senders, and mixed states.
rationale: The primary commit adds a differential equivalence harness whose comments state the invariant directly and whose cases compare the builder predicate against a direct translation of verifier nonce-lock phases across no jobs, settled jobs, fulfillment transactions, non-fulfillment locked senders, mixed settlement state, and many-job scenarios. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For a sender, transaction hash, and AsyncJobTracker state snapshot, `should_skip_sender_locked_tx` must return true exactly when the verifier nonce-lock logic would reject a block containing that transaction from that sender.
suggested_falsification_tests: Mutate either builder sender-lock logic or verifier nonce-lock logic so one allows a sender with an unsettled non-fulfillment job while the other rejects it, then run the differential harness and assert it fails.
summary: Builder and verifier nonce-lock decisions are covered by the shared predicate and differential harness in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Mutate should_skip_sender_locked_tx to allow a non-fulfillment transaction for a sender with an unsettled active job; the registry differential tests should fail against verifier_would_reject.
title: Builder and verifier nonce-lock decisions agree
